label: macOS · Apple M5 · bare metal

platform.version: Darwin Kernel Version 25.1.0: Mon Oct 20 19:34:03 PDT 2025; root:xnu-12377.41.6~2/RELEASE_ARM64_T8142

measurement.thermal: Note: No thermal warning level has been recorded Note: No performance warning level has been recorded Note: No CPU power status has been recorded
measurement.note: macOS does not expose governor/isolcpus/SMT toggles; RT via Mach time-constraint policy only (soft-RT). Use Linux + isolcpus for hard bounds.
uncontrolled_noise: macOS: no core isolation / governor control (soft-RT only)

results.name: dispatch+sync swapover (unified memory)
results.note: irreducible per-offload cost; unified memory (no PCIe). vs 83 ns bus hop / 12 µs ML-KEM — GPU disqualified for the latency path

results.name: throughput vs CPU
results.note: CPU wins (swapover not amortized); batch=4,096 f32 = 16 KiB

results.name: throughput vs CPU
results.note: CPU wins (swapover not amortized); batch=65,536 f32 = 256 KiB

results.name: throughput vs CPU
results.note: CPU wins (swapover not amortized); batch=262,144 f32 = 1024 KiB

results.name: throughput vs CPU
results.note: GPU wins; batch=1,048,576 f32 = 4096 KiB

results.name: throughput vs CPU
results.note: GPU wins; batch=4,194,304 f32 = 16384 KiB

results.name: throughput vs CPU
results.note: GPU wins; batch=16,777,216 f32 = 65536 KiB

results.name: crossover batch (GPU starts winning)
results.note: GPU offload pays off above ~1,048,576 f32 elems (~4096 KiB). At 1 KiB/stream that is ~4,096 concurrent streams — i.e. GPU is a SWARM/MESH/high-density-electrode scalability tool, not a per-node one

metrics.result:
determinism capabilities — Apple M5  (Darwin/arm64, 10 cores)
  core_isolation   False
  preempt_rt       False
  cache_pseudolock False
  hugepages        False
  umwait           False
  wfe              True
  time_constraint  True
  constant_tsc     True
  hedge_capable    True
  numa_nodes       1
  wait_strategy   wfe
recommendations:
  - macOS ceiling ~99.8%: no core isolation / cache-lock

note: Measured on this host only. Numbers are platform/CPU/build-specific. Re-run the identical script on each target OS to compare. Skipped rows indicate a capability/dependency absent on THIS platform, not a failure.
